mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that is flat-to-falling relative to trailing 3-month average signals reduced discount-rate pressure on equity valuations, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20d forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns within the following month as forward earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is not significantly different from unconditional Rank IC (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with industrial production growth (INDPRO) jointly proxy real economic momentum; when both accelerate together the combined signal captures broad-based expansion that is not detectable from either series alone, supporting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/INDPRO acceleration signal produces Rank IC no greater than the max of individual single-series Rank ICs over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated, further rate stability or increases amplify equity downside risk over short horizons because tightening-cycle valuation compression dominates; outside this regime the same signal is neutral or reversed.
failure_condition: Within the triggered regime, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects durable labor demand and household income growth that supports consumption and corporate earnings, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.10) with trailing 6-month PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that triggers rapid downward revisions to near-term corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC on triggered subset is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) weakness combined with a rising FEDFUNDS rate jointly signal that monetary tightening is actively suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand; the interaction of falling HOUST and rising FEDFUNDS predicts negative equity returns more reliably than either series alone over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-decline/FEDFUNDS-rise signal fails to outperform the better of the two single-series signals in Rank IC over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where UNRATE is near multi-decade lows (below 4%), a deceleration in PAYEMS growth signals imminent labor market turning points that precede equity market weakness over a quarterly horizon; in early-cycle regimes with UNRATE above 6% the same deceleration is not predictive.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates by > 1pp
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Conditional on the trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly lower than unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect is equally present when UNRATE exceeds 6%, indicating no regime dependence.
